enrichment.summary: Kyle records a day-late Boulder vlog describing his new world-building project he calls 'Boulder Rome' (part of a project called Sanctum Monium), where he mentally overlays ancient Rome's layout onto the real geography of Boulder, Colorado to create an imaginative personal world. He also recounts driving over Flagstaff mountain and realizing his prior Google Earth study let him recognize the terrain immediately.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: one of the most exciting things a person can do
enhances imagination and thinking about different worlds
studying Street View and Earth beforehand means you already know a place when you visit it in real life

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Living in Boulder, he forgot to do his video on November 13th, 2016, because he had to leave early for work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had to run out early because his coworkers texted him saying they would arrive early to pick him up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to work on his new project, Sanctum Monium, the next day since he doesn't work on Wednesdays.

claims.subject: Sanctum Monium
claims.claim: His new project is the most exciting thing a person could ever do, especially if they're high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He recalls listening to a history audiobook that mentioned people in the 1100s smoking weed in the Middle East and enjoying it because it made them think of different worlds.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When he gets really high, he likes thinking of different worlds.

claims.subject: Boulder Rome
claims.claim: He describes a concept called 'Boulder Rome,' where you rebuild Rome on top of Boulder, creating a third, imaginary place.

claims.subject: Boulder Rome
claims.claim: It's a cartoon-like place where you can create your own world using existing Boulder roads and buildings as a template.

claims.subject: Boulder Rome
claims.claim: The idea is to merge the historical and cultural elements of Rome with Boulder, making it a personalized, imaginative space.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to work on this project for a couple of weeks before returning to other projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is personalizing the project for himself, starting with mapping out circles radiating from his house.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is memorizing distances, like five plumbers (possibly a typo for 'blocks') from his house being 10 kilometers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He drove in the mountains today, around Flagstaff, and recognized the area because he had memorized it using Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He felt like he had entered Google Earth Street View because he had studied the area so thoroughly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He finds it really cool to memorize locations using Google Earth and then recognize them in real life.
